page.title: Marbella Yacht Charter Minimum Age Policy: Who Can Sail

data.meta_description: Learn the marbella yacht charter minimum age policy. From age 2 on private charters to 18+ for rentals, our guide covers who can sail from Puerto Banús.
data.summary: This guide explains the marbella yacht charter minimum age policy, covering age limits for passengers, skippers, and rentals. It provides clear rules for families and groups chartering boats from Puerto Banús.
key_facts: Minimum age for passengers on private charters: 2 years old.
Minimum age to rent a boat without skipper: 18 years old with valid licence.
Children under 12 must wear life jackets at all times on deck.
All our charters include a licensed skipper, so no licence needed for guests.
Maximum pax varies by boat: from 5 (Dubhe, Mariah SX21) to 12 (Mangusta 80, Ferretti 94).
No upper age limit for passengers, but mobility may affect access on certain boats.
Infants under 2 are not permitted on any charter for safety reasons.
All charters depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella.

faq.q: What is the minimum age for a yacht charter in Marbella?
faq.a: The minimum age for passengers on private charters is 2 years old. Infants under 2 are not permitted due to safety regulations. Children aged 2–11 are welcome but must wear life jackets on deck and be supervised by an adult at all times.

faq.q: Can teenagers charter a boat without an adult in Marbella?
faq.a: No. Anyone under 18 cannot rent a boat or be the primary booking holder. A responsible adult aged 18+ must be present on the charter and sign the contract. Teenagers are welcome as passengers when accompanied by a parent or guardian.

faq.q: Do I need a boating licence to charter a yacht in Marbella?
faq.a: No. All our charters include a licensed skipper, so no licence is required for guests. You simply relax and enjoy the trip. If you wish to rent a boat without a skipper, you must be 18+ and hold a valid Spanish or international boating licence.

faq.q: Is there a maximum age for passengers on Marbella yacht charters?
faq.a: There is no upper age limit. However, some boats have steep steps or limited accessibility. We recommend older guests or those with mobility concerns choose a boat with easy boarding, such as the Azimut 58 Flybridge or Ferretti 94, and inform us in advance.

faq.q: Are life jackets provided for children on your charters?
faq.a: Yes, we provide life jackets for all passengers, including children. Kids under 12 must wear them at all times while on deck. We also carry infant life jackets for children aged 2–5. Please let us know the ages of your children when booking.

faq.q: Can I bring a baby under 2 on a private charter?
faq.a: No. For safety reasons, infants under 2 years old are not permitted on any of our charters, even on private bookings. This policy applies to all boats in our fleet, regardless of size or duration.

faq.q: What happens if a guest is under 18 and we don't have an adult?
faq.a: The charter cannot proceed. All bookings require a responsible adult aged 18+ to be present on the boat and sign the liability waiver. If you are under 18, you must be accompanied by a parent, guardian, or another adult over 18.

faq.q: Do you offer family-friendly charters for kids in Marbella?
faq.a: Absolutely. Families are welcome. Boats like the Lagoon 380 (catamaran) and Astondoa 40 are popular with families due to stable decks and shaded areas. We provide soft drinks, snacks, and can arrange kid-friendly catering on request. Just let us know when booking.

data.body_html:
Planning a yacht charter in Marbella with family or friends? One of the first questions you'll ask is about the marbella yacht charter minimum age policy. Whether you're bringing toddlers, teenagers, or elderly parents, knowing who can sail is essential for a smooth day on the water. At Boat Rental Marbella, all our charters depart from Puerto Banús and include a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, and insurance. Here's everything you need to know about age limits, safety rules, and requirements.

Minimum Age for Passengers

The minimum age for passengers on any private charter in Marbella is 2 years old. Infants under 2 are not permitted for safety reasons. Children aged 2 to 11 are welcome but must wear a life jacket at all times while on deck. We provide life jackets for all sizes, including toddlers. For boats like the Dubhe (5 pax) or Mariah SX21 (5 pax), space is limited, so consider larger vessels like the Mangusta 80 (12 pax) or Ferretti 94 (12 pax) for family groups.

Minimum Age for Renters and Skippers

To book a yacht charter, the primary renter must be 18 years or older. A valid ID or passport is required at check-in. If you plan to rent a boat without a skipper (self-drive), you need a valid boating licence recognised in Spain and be 18+. However, all our charters come with a licensed skipper, so no licence is needed—just relax and enjoy the Costa del Sol coastline.

Age Rules for Different Boat Types

Our fleet includes boats of various sizes and layouts, each with its own suitability for different age groups. Here's a quick overview:

BoatMax PaxBest for
Dubhe (8m)5Couples or small families with older kids
Astondoa 40 (12.5m)9Families with children (stable deck)
Lagoon 380 (11.6m)10Families, catamaran stability
Mangusta 80 (24m)12Large groups, all ages
Ferretti 94 (29m)12Luxury, elderly-friendly boarding

For elderly guests or those with mobility issues, the Azimut 58 Flybridge or Ferretti 94 offer easier boarding and more space. Always inform us when booking so we can recommend the best option.

Safety Requirements for Children

Spanish maritime law requires that children under 12 wear life jackets on deck at all times. Our crew will provide properly sized jackets and brief all guests on safety procedures. We also recommend sun protection (SPF 50+, hats, UV clothing) as the sun on the Mediterranean can be intense, even in spring. The water temperature off Marbella ranges from 16°C in April to 24°C in August.

Booking with Infants or Toddlers

As noted, infants under 2 are not permitted. For toddlers aged 2–5, we provide infant life jackets and can arrange kid-friendly snacks (fruit, juice boxes) on request. Boats with shaded areas, like the Lagoon 380 catamaran, are ideal for keeping little ones cool. All charters include soft drinks and water, so no need to bring your own.

What About Elderly Passengers?

There is no maximum age for passengers. However, some boats have steep gangways or ladder access. We recommend the Maiora 26m or Canados 86 for their wide boarding platforms and stable rides. If a guest has limited mobility, please call us on WhatsApp before booking to ensure the boat is suitable. The crew can assist with boarding but cannot carry passengers.

Group Bookings and Mixed Ages

Planning a birthday, wedding, or corporate event with a mix of ages? Our larger boats like the Mangusta 80 (12 pax) or Ferretti 94 (12 pax) are perfect. All guests must follow the same age rules. For example, a group with a 3-year-old and a 70-year-old is fine—just ensure the child wears a life jacket and the elder can board safely. We can also arrange catering for all ages.

Frequently asked questions

What is the minimum age for a yacht charter in Marbella?

The minimum age for passengers on private charters is 2 years old. Infants under 2 are not permitted due to safety regulations. Children aged 2–11 are welcome but must wear life jackets on deck and be supervised by an adult at all times.

Can teenagers charter a boat without an adult in Marbella?

No. Anyone under 18 cannot rent a boat or be the primary booking holder. A responsible adult aged 18+ must be present on the charter and sign the contract. Teenagers are welcome as passengers when accompanied by a parent or guardian.

Do I need a boating licence to charter a yacht in Marbella?

No. All our charters include a licensed skipper, so no licence is required for guests. You simply relax and enjoy the trip. If you wish to rent a boat without a skipper, you must be 18+ and hold a valid Spanish or international boating licence.

Is there a maximum age for passengers on Marbella yacht charters?

There is no upper age limit. However, some boats have steep steps or limited accessibility. We recommend older guests or those with mobility concerns choose a boat with easy boarding, such as the Azimut 58 Flybridge or Ferretti 94, and inform us in advance.

Are life jackets provided for children on your charters?

Yes, we provide life jackets for all passengers, including children. Kids under 12 must wear them at all times while on deck. We also carry infant life jackets for children aged 2–5. Please let us know the ages of your children when booking.

Can I bring a baby under 2 on a private charter?

No. For safety reasons, infants under 2 years old are not permitted on any of our charters, even on private bookings. This policy applies to all boats in our fleet, regardless of size or duration.

What happens if a guest is under 18 and we don't have an adult?

The charter cannot proceed. All bookings require a responsible adult aged 18+ to be present on the boat and sign the liability waiver. If you are under 18, you must be accompanied by a parent, guardian, or another adult over 18.

Do you offer family-friendly charters for kids in Marbella?

Absolutely. Families are welcome. Boats like the Lagoon 380 (catamaran) and Astondoa 40 are popular with families due to stable decks and shaded areas. We provide soft drinks, snacks, and can arrange kid-friendly catering on request. Just let us know when booking.
